channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History — 48 replies — 192.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-05-13 19:15 UTC

OP

Historic moment as Chainlink services have been selected to be used by the DTCC! This is monumental to the crypto and blockchain industry. They even pinned the tweet! Wow, to think how far we have come as an industry.

Early replies (first 3)

62224616
Is this good or bad?

62224627
when are people going to wake up and realize that chainlink is the only crypto that matters? when will the fucking price finally go up?

62224642
>>62224627
I think you honestly really needed the spoon feeding from autists here back in 2017 to gather the confidence we have in Link. By the time Link got on the crypto map in 2020 or whenever people were cemented in their cliques for whatever coin they believed in. Most of the crypto space are normalfaggots. I’ve gotten a handful of people to buy, but you can never tell them to go all in cuz they are not emotionally invested in link. I got6 people to invest 300, 300, 700, and 500 and 400 and 1700. Almost all of them

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224825
>>62224813
Anon, before OCR tokens were given as a subsidy by CLL.
>The subscription pays for the Link tokens going to the nodes
You have no idea how these subscriptions are negotiated or paid. 90% of the time the industry pays itself using stablecoins.
>retard
let's try to be civil yeah

62224829
>>62224825
>they don't use tokens
>ok they use tokens but they're subsidies

begone Jew

62224863
>>62224577
What is sui and make it stacks for link. I'm poor and have 110 AIGMI?

62224958
>>62224863
You need 890 more to avoid suicidal thoughts.
You need 9,890 more to make it.
Have a nice day.

62224974
>>62224863
>>62224958
Listen cuckolds, there isn't a SINGLE person who has /madeit/ with Chainlink. All the talks about make it stacks turned out to be complete bullshit. Linkies with 20k stacks barely have 200k after 9 years of bagholding

Go back to Twitter or whatever shithole you crawled out of

62225022
>>62224974
We know the speculative crypto market is retarded.
That's why all of crypto is in the gutter and stocks are at ATH.

62225026
>>62224974
>Go back to Twitter or whatever shithole you crawled out of
you're blocked there lol

62225060
>>62224577
this is a biggie for the baggies

62225084
>>62225060
>>62224974
keep seething retard niggers. production in october to december this year

62225091
>>62224651
Ask an AI to explain what the DTCC does like you are 5, because you are

62225100
>>62224577
Um price... and also chart?
>>62224749

62225125
>>62225100
>Chainlink Proof of Concept with SWIFT
>poc? Price and chart? LOLOLOL
>Chainlink Smart NAV pilot with DTCC
>muh PILOTT
>Chainlink Production Integration into DTCC
>muh... muh price and chart
<--- you are here
>Chainlink Production Integration into SWIFT

62225173
As usual, lots of faggots in here who dont care about chainlink, spending all their time researching and talking about chainlink. I do the same thing with all the stuff I hate too, I obsess over it and attack it at any mention of it online.

62225178
>>62224616
Bad for the price of 1 LINK

62225181
>>62224644
Why would they not care, wtf

62225215
>>62225100
whew, thanks asswipe i really needed that, now i feel like i'm back to reality, i ain't sellin, i'm just gonna baghold another decade i guess

62225318
>>62224577
Chainlink? Now that’s a name I’ve haven’t heard in a long long time I had some when I was a young man.

62225326
WOW! This is huge!

Now let me just check the price and the chart…oh…

62225449
>>62225181
because the conversation about link's potential is like schizo babble

62226061
>>62224577
I thought this happened like 2 years ago..

62226079
>>62225318
im 25y and will be a millionaire before 30 with my linkstack, stay seething

62226196
>>62224749
>9 years of bagholding
Hey cuckold, check the price :)

62226241
>>62226196
thx i just checked oh boy! am I le excite!

62226533
>*dumps*

62227022
can I get an honest and unbiased QRD on link?

62227259
>>62227022
No, I don't think you can. Read the white papers and think for yourself. That'll probably be the closest you can get. Everyone else who would answer you is emotionally invested one way or the other. Me? I think it's the only project that matters and that it'll make me the equivalent of a billionaire one day. It's the next iteration of the internet, just as DLT was the next iteration of the computer.

62227290
>>62227022
No.

62227293
>>62224577
>dumps to single digits

62227302
>>62224749
Bag size?

62227385
>>62227022
Fundamentally, I’ve noticed a lot of new posters in the last 69hr (SIXTY NINE) asking about Chainlink and just wanted to warn you. Chainlink is shilled HEAVILY here and it’s very hard to understand so this background is crucial to ensure you stay safe.

Basically, a bunch of RACIST WHITE CIS MALE 4channers tried to force it as /ourcoin/, during the presale ico phase of chainlink there was a minimum requirement of 300eth to enter the presale.

Coin continued to get shilled and pumped up and hyped for the sibios event that link was attended, whole event turned out to be a flop chainlink had a presentation in a room of like 18 people (IT WAS BESIDE THE FUCKING TOILETS FOR FUCK'S SAKES, YOU COULD HEAR THEM FLUSHING), literally no news or partnership came from the event.

Now during this alt bull run lots of anons and took advantage of this and shilling this coin to all the new money and newfags that joined in december and don't know this story.
The coin is HEAVILY manipulated by a literal TRANNY DISCORD who pump and dump this coin more than their boyfriends pump and dump hot cum into their asses, and the supply is dried up from huge fucking chink whales (xi jinping is rumoured to hold link) who accumulated below ICO price to create a artificially lower supply.

In regards to actual project that chainlink aiming to achieve it's nothing more than a basic json parser (OH MY GOD ITS JSON PARSER...) for smart contracts (LOL HOW CAN A CONTRACT BE "SMART"???), would take like a day to add to ethereum by itself.. literally making links whole concept pointless and definitely no need for a token. You're literally just buying a wh*tepaper.

They only have 2 devs who are literal pajeets who are payed in big macs (non beef) and Surgay NAZAJEET is a literal psychologist (trusting a shrink? is he gonna tell us to go clean our room next?). So FUNDAMENTALLY, it's a scam, ok thanks.

Now go do the needful and fundamentally buy a real coin with fundamentals like XRP.

item.title: Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k)… — 20 replies — 40.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k)…

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-13 18:46 UTC

OP

Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k) and focus on finishing my masters and not huffing fumes in traffic?


I feel like i wont regret giving up wfh later in life but I'll be miserable for the next 3 years.. I feel like im fucked either way.

Early replies (first 3)

62222340
>>62222336
Meant to say later in life like 3 years later when I quit cuz I cant go in the office my while life, id rather be dead. But it might be worth it to suck it up for a few years

62222349
What's the masters in? This is the hinge the decision should be on.

62222450
>>62222336
Consider how much money you would spend on gas commuting everyday for a year, and consider that gas could still go up another 50% from today's prices. So you know that you are spending $3.5k — 5.2k in gas this year citing today's average American gas price for calculations. Is office life worth this sacrifice?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62222880
>>62222336
take the 50% raise wtf

62222939
how long is the gommute

62222960
please kill yourself

62223191
>>62222349
Electrical Engineering

62223303
>>62222939
The goymute is 42 miles so I would have to move or get a tesla with fsd

62223366
Things are about to get COVID style unstable,band unemployment is at 30%. I'd go with whichever job is less likely to terminate early. Any steady paycheck is better than no paycheck.

62223378
>>62223303
I personally would not want to spend 2 hrs a day commuting. That time could be spent doing sports, dating, another side hustle that I really like, etc.

62223393
>>62223303
I would not unless you are open to moving closer.
>>62223366
Also this

I would probably kms if I had to do an actual rigorous masters while working full time in office desu

62223417
>>62222880
Ikr, yet a lot of people here and a big part of myself really want work life balance but 50 percent raise should be a no brainer

62223423
>>62223378
I cant afford to date on 105k a year also im an engineer so what dates?

62223427
>>62223393
I am thinking of killing myself. I am open to moving but its in a liberal hellhole. Gotta do what I gotta do.

62224325
>>62222336
Get a job that's no easily replaced by AI
If you commute buy a Tesla with fsd so you don't have to actively drive and spend the time doing something productive.

62224369
>>62222336
putting up with non whites in person is not worth anything

62224584
>>62223366
In two weeks right?

62227221
>>62224369
this. I'm in the same spot as OP and refuse to work in office. Pay me 50k I dont care. I make more from my investment portfolio daily than my job by far anyway

62227237
take the raise

wfh is comfy but you really become a piece of shit with the time

also you can cheat on your wife at work

62227270
>>62222336
>Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise
>Do I give up WFH

No. The answer is objectively and conclusively "no"

item.title: V shaped dip edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the movies >Gam… — 37 replies — 27.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
V shaped dip edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the movies >Gam…

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-05-13 17:56 UTC

OP

V shaped dip edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>NEW THREAD: Copy-paste the following when making a new thread:
https://pastebin.com/NyzbAsX4

Old >>62222475

Early replies (first 3)

62223155
So the GME baggies are getting fucked. Again lol

62223156
>NUGODS

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62223162
Quick lads! We've got minutes!
Minutes I tells ya!

62223163
>>62223141
>no /smg/ in title
This is becoming a regular thing

62223164
>>62223155
do gme shareholders go to gme stores? i cant remember the last time i saw one.

62223166
>>62223147
I don't trust banks, they will rat you out. I just cash out into gold whenever I hit the threshold.

62223169
>>62223163
It's almost as if he does it on purpose.

62223171
>futures

62223172
>>62223162
Burry really is just the average smg bear

62223177
>>62223162
Must be doomsday clock minutes

62223178
>>62223172
burry was right once

62223179
>>62223166
I've never been audited despite 7 years of claiming benefits. The only thing they have is an automatic verifier that checks some employment, but not all.

62223183
My stock did not go up today

But that's NOT because my stock went up 200% in the past two months and I'd highly visible. It's because there is a very conspiracy between the democrats, the EU, the market markets, the hedge funds and of course the terrible Jew to personally make me feel emotionally bad.

62223190
WEN shares trade at $7.90. get taken private at $10, 11, 12.

decent profit right there /smg/

62223193
i refuse to use this thread, someone bake another one

62223195
>>62223141

No /smg/

Real thread

>>62223180
>>62223180
>>62223180

62223205
Silver

62223206
>>62223193
it can get worse lol

62223220
>ai is a bubble

62223322
Buying ADBE

62223420
I never made a play on oil before, the closest I ever was, was when I heard Trump was sending a fleet enroute to Iran. I knew he'd start a war
but I didn't buy oil, even though I knew it would go up. It was at like 65$ or something when I was considering it. Maybe $70

62223446
>>62223420
I remember people posting about it and not listening which is annoying

62223449
>>62223420
Oil was fun on the first run-up. Danganasspostinganon correctly called the peak of $120. If only I immediately full ported back into stocks after I sold it and didn't just call it a day

62223501
Yep, selling half

62223513
>>62223501
Okay

62223632
Buy HANTA coin if you want to live.
Easy 2-3x, with a 200k% lottery ticket

62223639
>>62223141
If the stock market crashes on my payday I'm going to lose my mind buying up as much as I can

62223978
Failing my economic class to browse biz

62224109
>>62224082
why?

62226852
>>62223141
So if the tech stocks that make up 40% of the market decline 80% on average, then the market as a whole should be expected to go down ~30%, right?
So I should start buying once it's 30% down from whatever the high ends up being? Is that a good plan?

62227003
>forgot to put smg in the title
>wow there's like no volume on the smg thread
>this means something

item.title: >redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD >everyone here is poo… — 34 replies — 23.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD >everyone here is poo…

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-05-13 17:49 UTC

OP

>redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD
>everyone here is poor as fuck

Early replies (first 3)

62208450
just goes to show 4chan is much more retarded than >leddit :^)

62208458
only a few more weeks until I can post on reddit

62208475
>>62208412
We were targeted specifically by kikes to be made poor

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208497
>>62208495
How would you even begin to prove such a baseless assertion? You think redditors invented dollar cost averaging, portfolio diversity, or having optimism about tech industries?

62208501
>>62208497
no way to prove it, i have simply observed the average frequency of gains and reddit is the clear winner

/biz/ however won with the OG LINK marines

62208528
>>62208412
>>everyone here is poor as fuck
Everyone here is the most retarded kind of skeptic, bagholder fudding new investments, or bear who thinks the market is going back to 30k on the DJIA for nk reason.

62208530
>>62208501
>>62208497
>>62208475
Reddit doesn't ruthlessly purge The Next Big thing until it's too late to make sizable gains, it doesn't have low IQ browns phoneposting "SAAR BUY THE TOP OF A MASSIVE CANDLE" it doesn't have 50yo xoomers fudding every good investment becuase "muh tech bubble was bad" 20 years ago.

62208534
>>62208528
kek you are poor

62208537
>>62208458
my ip is range banned from reddit permanently

62208538
Don't kid yourselves. Crypto is in the beginnings of a decade long bear market. /pmg/fags will get the final laugh.

62208544
>>62208528
you keep taking my screenshot. I want to strangle you

62208568
>>62208530
Reddit does, in fact, purge a lot of its comments. It is also filled to the brim with botposting. Don't know why you'd treat their circlejerk as more valuable than any other random shitpost you read online.

>>62208544
I should take it and use it as mine too.

62208833
>>62208412
I see WSB getting filthy rich meanwhile /biz/ chuds are here bagholding chainlink and "investing" in rocks

62209921
>>62208412
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62209936
>>62208501
the weird part here is we have bears that never post ANY gains LOL

62211249
>>62208537
based
>>62209921
go post that over there

62213463
>>62208412
I'm doing ok

62217703
>>62208412
why is this thread still up its been 2 days wtf

62218664
>>62209921
>take a $1,000,000 loan

Suddenly a millionaire.

62218683
>>62213463
give me 100k and you'll fix my life

62218698
>>62208530
I remember the dot com crash
>/b/ told me pets.com the make it stock

62218792
>>62208458
So what will you do with the money once it reaches 1 million, will it be enough for you?

I wont be satisfied until I reach at least 3 million which is 10-12 years away at a minimum.

62218804
>>62208530
reddit has loser bears too

62221909
if I were to sell everything today I'd have 20K€

62222773
>>62221909
What were you doing for the past 10 years? if you saved 25-30k a year youd have at least over 400k by now from the compounded interest.

62222950
>>62218792
I already have over a million. I am an oldfag already, so I am aiming for 2.5 or so before I consider retiring. with retirement accounts and cash I am around 1.6. realistically if I retire early my biggest cost will be health insurance. I don’t want to risk going without in my late 40s and 50s.

62222978
Fine. I give up. This place is a total shit hole and I’ll never make it by browsing this total brown cesspit. What subreddits do I go to make money?

62223083
>>62222950
Ill live long. This is my affirmation. I will live long, healthy, and get to 3 million dollars by the time I'm 35.

62223085
>>62208495
retard doesnt know what survivorship bias is. I have 100% called the last 3 market bottoms because every reddit post was screaming about an imminent market collapse

62225427
>>62222773
>the past 10 years
been working for 3, was a neet before
>25-30k
I make that a year before taxes, you probably missed the euro sign

62225585
>>62209936
I have nothing

62226924
>>62208412
Fuggg I was thinking of buying AMD a few years ago when NVIDIA was going apeshit, but I did some googling and something I read convinced me not to. Forgot what it was. Feels batman

62226971
>>62218683
No

item.title: XMR — 22 replies — 16.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
XMR

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-13 17:56 UTC

OP

Delisted at 160, currently at 410

Early replies (first 3)

62213668
Nobody gives two shits about crapto in general. You are a baggie trying to find a greater fool, you were the greatest fool

62213696
>>62213668
You are cattle.

62213702
>>62213696
No, I am cashflow that is never going to have a demand for your bags. No matter what you baggies advertise

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62214009
>>62213668
I made it through XMR, it's an excellent opportunity for anyone who listens.

62216189
I like the coin

62216387
>>62214009
Same I dumped on newbaggies when we hit 800. Years of accoomoolating paid off.

62216481
>>62213665
Yeah but what can I buy with it? Where do I sell it for fiat?

62217118
>>62216481
Check monerica, you can buy pretty much anything.

62217182
>>62216481
>Yeah but what can I buy with it?
Anything. I'm currently buying work supplies for my business with XMR. That compounded with how much I've made my business is basically free and I'm making a couple thousand dollars a week.

62222692
>>62214009
based
sound money, safe mode

62222705
>>62217182
based
I've been shilling it in Lebanon, and trying to educate everyone
fuck central banks
fuck fiat
(((keynesians))) deserve the rope

https://www.france24.com/en/live-news/20210601-lebanon-crisis-among-world-s-worst-since-1850s-world-bank

never forget

62222769
>>62222705
Glad to see you're still around

62222781
>>62222769
thank you my friend
we have a saying here, roughly translates to:
if God is with us, who can be against us

62222813
>>62222781
Lovely, could've come straight from the apodeipnon

62223307
>>62222705
Fuck Israel too

62223522
How do I buy this dogshit and bury it in a ledger?
There was the wagyu hyperliquid thing but I don't trust that nigger

62223640
>>62223307
based

if you got some extra XMR to send I wouldn't mind
>>62214009
only asking cause I saw this
>>62222813
Christ is king

62223742
>>62223640
honestly im desperate enough to ask lel

XMR: 893qvnGketXV72jLmcDNhSWQgMQvzP4ab71HdaWMPrBrittG5PPfVrZcFKNYHv9UfXRDBkzvzNSc5bn7m7nJA6taKJ37gQW

62224485
>>62223742
When we hit 500 I'll send 0.1 XMR, screenshot this

62226010
I like the coin

62226990
>>62224485
thank you friend

62227005
newfags still haven't learned what a monero spike means

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 89 replies — 30.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-05-13 16:18 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62209287
>>62208072
days over? You're lying

62209291
>>62209260
Hodl

62209304
>>62195275
BTC dominance still looks too strong for a full alt run right now.

62209376
>>62207965
Let's see how it goes

62209379
>>62204588
So are you into btc?

62209385
Bitcoin is getting fucked

62209525
>>62209385
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Just btc

62209590
People pretending fundamentals matter in crypto will never stop being funny.

62209854
>>62195275
Everyone becomes a macro analyst after holding one red candle lmao

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

62211194
>>62207855
I've got some hbar in my portfolio

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

62211903
>>62195275
Everyone moved to BTC of course, I dumped my ETH for BTC too, I used to hold BTC because of staking but now I can stake BTC natively thanks to TBV and the likes

62211915
>>62195275
I’d rather keep stacking BTC, then put it to work via native staking / yield layers (like Babylon-style setups) instead of chasing dead charts and hoping for a narrative pump.

62211928
>>62209385
Ok as you say so

62211932
>>62211429
Hbar is okay to hold and buy

62212034
>>62195275
More potential upside on alts for the next six months. Specifically memes right now.

62213245
>>62211440
What's your price target anon?

62214696
>>62211932
All in or?

62216973
>>62211915
It's worth considering anon

62216987
>>62195275

Polkadot

62220595
>>62209854
Are you sure?

62224657
>>62216987
Why polkadot?

62224666
cny/usd is > .147 now w/trump headed to china. every penny that can chase risk on is gonna get horned up immediately. The ONLY thing the US has left is the dollar as a w3@p0n. Plus, i can't afford any more makers mark bottles unless my alts start pumping w/haste, so...

62224894
>>62224666
Crazy

62224972
>>62224666
We're cooked

62226444
>>62224666
Risk-on driven by geopolitics, liquidity, and alcoholism. Peak crypto posting.

item.title: NEW TRUMP POST — 46 replies — 24.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
NEW TRUMP POST

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-05-13 17:23 UTC

OP

Bears are fucked

Early replies (first 3)

62224296
>>62224292
Digits and the plane crashes

62224311
>>62224292
>>62224296
Putin, you know what to do

62224318
>>62224311
Fucking RIP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224582
>>62224431
he didnt

62224592
>>62224292
New fagshit dropped oooooooooooooooooo

62224594
>>62224592
Sry forgot pic

62224597
>>62224292
>''american'' billionaires fly to China to beg in person for them to loose up their asses even by little
everything they possibly offer technologically and resource wise is already in China, unless they offer up (you) and chinamen will go for it for some reason. And Taiwan too but that's not a "deal" that's hanging up white flag and letting go of US influence in east asia

62224617
>>62224292
that's why I don't envy these CEOs that much. imagine having to share a private plane with smelly donald trump just to get humiliated in china by people who prepared 1000 times better for this meeting than the trump administration is capable of. all that while they certainly have "the cards" and while you have to agree with a dementia patient that the USA is outperforming everyone in everything. literally nobody wants to be on that trip.

62225118
>>62224318
Liberals are now actually believing digits on 4chan mean something.

Glorious.

62225176
Did he really say Tim Apple again kek

62225297
>>62224431
who invites a man before throwing him from a plane?

62225310
>>62225176
Tim Apple is hilarious.

62225504
>>62224292
>Tim Apple

Imagine being Tim Cook and being called Tim Apple hahahhaa what a fucking cuck

62225529
>Tim Apple
>Larry Blackrock
>Elon Tesla
>Jensen Nvidia

62225538
>>62224311
Ahhhhhhhh it’s fucking happening!!!!!!!!!!!

62225699
>>62224292
Him and Elon are friends again?

62225749
How like is to Nvidia reach 10 Trillion in Marketcap in the next 2 years?

62225825
>>62225699
the is no friendship in these circles, only loyalty. Elon learned his lesson.

62225943
>>62225297

They are expecting one of us in the wreckage brother.

62225964
>>62225699
I see you don't know how pro-wrestling works. Elon has done a "double turn" where he went from babyface to heel and now he is back to babyface.

62226072
>>62225964
this wise anon knows that if you're a smark who understands kayfabe, you're automatically fluent in politics

62226167
>>62224414
Right? This seems like some sort of last ditch effort, and almost a recognition that China "won" in some greater sense. Actually pretty disappointing we've reached this point, cowtowing to communists

62226338
>>62226167
Ironically "kowtowing" is a Chinese term

62226357
>>62224292
lol left wing media btfo again

62226359
how do i profit from this

62226366
>>62226359
Buy Nvidia I guess. He's clearly going to allow Jensen to sell to them

62226395
>>62226366
I checked the CEOs travelling with him. Why does he have a random small/mid-cap biotech company with him? Does anyone know anything about Illumina? Its only 22 billion mcap doing DNA sequencing

62226412
>>62226395
Theres a new plandemic remember. they're probably there to help China combine the bat flu with the hantavirus.

62226422
>>62224324
How do i get out of this chickenshit market?

62226424
>>62224292
is this why metals are going up

62226428
>>62225749
>all that bloated garbage

62226472
>>62226424

62226813

item.title: Cerebras IPO May 14th edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the mo… — 309 replies — 19.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Cerebras IPO May 14th edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the mo…

Open thread • Replies: 309 • Updated: 2026-05-13 03:02 UTC

OP

Cerebras IPO May 14th edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>NEW THREAD: Copy-paste the following when making a new thread:
https://pastebin.com/NyzbAsX4

PREVIOUS
https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/62217769/smg-stock-market-general#top

Early replies (first 3)

62218691
> futures

62218697
AVGO, SITM, DVN & GIS
WY to 30 eventually

62218700
INTC ain’t stopping until 2T in market cap. Believe it (dattebayo)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62219835
>>62219564
I'm charging it out at as a variation with a floating average for larger clients.
And an inflated fixed cost average for smaller clients.

It took a lot of negotiations initially but the reality set in to developers.
My diesel costs per month went from $30k to nearly $80k. I have staff vehicles as well as machines

62219848
>>62219835
>>62219570
for

62219849
>>62218685
Anyone buying this IPO?

I don't want to be a goyim.

62219854
I have an important question

With ibkr

can you buy fractional shares with Japanese stocks? By fractional, I mean, can you buy 1 share? It's hard for me to hit my exact balance with Japanese stocks when I have to buy 100 lot shares. I know ibkr used to require you to buy 100x lots of Japanese shares. I just wonder if they let you buy 1 shares, instead of forcing 100. Its hard to weight this shit when 100 shares of some JP companies is $50,000 lol

62219860
>>62219854
No.
The asian Stock Exchanges have minimums

62219876
>>62219860
Are you sure? Because I checked online, and some sources said yes, and others no

Did you try to buy 1 JP shares on IBKR in the last year or less?

62219878
>>62219860
And by the way, it doesnt matter if the asian stock exchanges require minimums if the broker does fractional shares. I want to know if IBKR specifically does fractional shares for JP stocks.

62219882
>>62219854
If you're a Euro, use Trading 212

62219884
>>62219876
>Did you try to buy 1 JP shares on IBKR in the last year or less?
How about right now?

62219889
now the market wants to dip

62219892
>>62219882
>>62219860
>>62219884
But Gemini says yes

62219894
>>62219889

62219897
>>62219892
>But Gemini says yes
Why are you asking AI?
And why are you asking us?
You can either do it or you can't, and you can't.

62219906
>>62219884
Check your settings in trading permissions. See if you have fractional shares enabled.

62219909
>>62219906
No, you.

62219914
>>62219909
Please

62219922
>>62219909
If you do it, Ill tell you what JP tickers I want to buy

62219931
>>62219922
You need an account registered as "expert trader" to unlock the option.
So if you want to try it out, you'll have to lie and claim you're an expert with over 10 years experience

62219938
>>62219931
>He isn't an expert with over 10 years experience

NGMI

62219986
>>62219931
why wont you just lie?

62220010
>>62219986
Because an investor only has so many lies in a day and he needs to save them for explaining to the wife whete all their money went.

62220032
>>62220010
thats bullshit
but I believe it

62220034
>>62219986
Because I told the truth about my experience when I opened the account. Now I have to make up some lie to my broker how I instantly gained 8 years experience to show an anonymous frog whether he can buy fractional anime shares.

62220186
why are prediction markets legal? It's literally gambling, and zero sum

62224103
why didn't anons kill the thread?

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 29 replies — 9.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-05-13 16:16 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62220146
I have 5 BTC in cold storage. Am I gonna make it??

62220158
>>62220146
definitely. but also because of your eth stack, with just 5 BTC no.

62221733
>>62219942
Its ok. I have monkey NFTs. They are the future

62222391
>>62219945
noisee!!! Show me your proof anon

62222489
>>62219942
I hold
0.24 BTC
6k XRP
12 LTC

AIGMI?

62222594
I'm afraid of quantum computing

62223021
>>62219942
I have 1 Bitcoin and it doesn't feel like enough. It takes so much for it to go over 100k

62223087
>>62222489
no, that portfolio is far too retarded. kys

62223238
>>62219995
You can aim more if you want

62224650
>>62220027
You should also keep accumulating anon

62224655
dump it to $30k and the risk reward looks decent..

62224679
The backlash of gen z faggots realizing they slandered their best years not actually living and frugally tormenting themselves buying imaginary totally wortless fugazi will be glorious.

62224895
>>62224655
I will buy more btc if it dumps to that 30k

62224905
>>62219942
I'm at 0.02, am I cooked ? I keep piling up the satoshis but it's so slow.

62224933
>>62224905
You're nigger

62224971
>>62224679
Wtf are you saying poor man?

62224973
>>62223021
If it performs half as well as it did in the past 10 years for the next 10 years, it’ll easily hit $1,000,000. Institutional adoption has just begun. Dont give up now, anon.

62224979
if I ignored bit-tards, worked overtime and accumulated AI stocks like Nvidia, I would have already made it.

62225000
>>62222489
You win the prize for gayest portfolio anon

62225031
>>62224971
Time is money, faggot.

62225044
>>62224650
yeah, im waiting for a pullback i think we're going back to 76-78k soon. gay faggot BTC will do that for sure.

62225050
>>62224933
just a Europoor

62225117
>>62219942
I am new I will rotate into it.

62225388
>>62219942
What institutions are vacuuming up BTC??

62225995
>>62224895
Wouldn’t even mind that scenario. Stack more, keep self custody, and let the asset become productive over time instead of just sitting there.

62226434
>>62221733
The jpeg economy will rise again?

item.title: Taxes Thread — 21 replies — 9.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Taxes Thread

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-13 17:05 UTC

OP

Can any USA tax lads please go more in depth and in detail about how to set up a tax efficient business+trust?

Particularly how to be the most tax efficient with a business that is also protected by within a trust? S-Corp or C-Corp? Both in the same state? Offshore? How do you avoid double taxation?

Most importantly, how would you invest personal and business income in long term stocks that you select?

How would you deal with income you got from an actual job somewhere which is not part of the your business but that you wanted protected by a trust and invested in your choice of stocks?

What would all the target tax rates be?

Please detail everything. Provide tips and cover all risks, etc. I have tried studying this on my own but the scenario I want in which all income from business and personal in custom selected stocks while protected by trust and taxed as small as possible, complicates things.

Early replies (first 3)

62218156
>>62218140
consult a great tax attorney and a cpa

62218211
>>62218140
if you make a personal s-corp you can expense everything in CA like paying for your own health insurance for example but you pay a minimum franchise tax of $800 even if you made nothing so not great for side-hustles owned by me that made nothing

62218357
>>62218140
Formula F1. If you can spare 10m, sponsor an F1 team and then expense your entire life

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62218371
>>62218140
Totally off-topic but it really makes me seethe that I’m really out here paying 25 percent of my income to pedophiles, then on top of that I get taxed on investments, purchasing anything, property, etc. Our ancestors tarred and feathered tax collectors over a 2 percent tax on stamps and now we work for three months out of the year for the government.

62218405
>>62218211
This is basically the grift. You set up an S-Corp and deduct everything as a “business expense.”

It is also illegal to claim a business deduction for personal expenses, but this never stopped black people on TikTok from expensing the lease payments on their Chryslers.

62218495
>>62218140
I hate redditors and normies and low iq's. Pic related is such a retarded analogy. The company gets tax deductions because those expenditures were related to income generation. A person can do the same thing by getting tax deductions on things deemed necessary for work or income generation. You spending your income on overpriced doordash is not related to income generation.

62218507
>>62218357
Yeah just pay 10m to save 4m in taxes goy. Not a very smart jew. This is only worth it if you were going to spend money to live that lifestyle anyway, or you needed the marketing regardless.

62218524
the business spent that 3.9B on salaries to all the retards making 60k

62218651
I paid like 95k in income tax then got clocked for 4k more when I did my taxes.

62218760
>>62218140
Unless you make serious cash, this is the best you can hope for.

62218844
>>62218495
huh? i can't work hungry

62218962
>>62218140
Talk to @TheJerzWay on X (he will require payment), but he's a pro. He'll probably also recommend getting your REPS and residency in Puerto Rico.

62219425
>>62218140
Just don't pay your taxes
Most of them go towards jews and the rest goes towards trying to bring in as many third world subhumans to replace you as possible
It is probably the most cucked thing you can do in the current age, especially in the U.S
Anyone who disagrees with this should respond with "I am a good goy cuck" because there's no rationalisation or argument to make what I'm saying any less true.

62219490
>>62218140
>That's not–
You don't pay taxes at all, do you?
You don't know what a Standard Deduction is, do you?
>File by May 15th
Yeah, whoever wrote this has never paid taxes in their life.
>>62218507
>This is only worth it if you were going to spend money to live that lifestyle anyway,
He was. They all do. This is "Hollywood accounting". But now he can use his business to fund it with no risk to his personal assets.

62219495
>>62218844
Your basic needs are covered by the standard deduction.

62220996
>>62218357
Good job shlomo!

62221525
>>62218140
Tell me you don't know the fucking difference between a W2 and a 1099 without telling me.

62223970
>>62218405
Ben affleck explains how to do this in the Accountant movie.

62224000
>>62218405
Yes, but ultimately this just lets you live an inflated lifestyle at a discount. If you don't really want a G wagon, even making it tax deductible doesn't really help because you've still got to pay for the non deductible amount. And if you don't have a business that lets you pretend like maybe you need it (ie. A real estate agent taking wealthy clients to see homes)

62224006
>>62218140
>60k income
>58k expenses
yeah no wonder the person who made this meme doesen't understand taxes

62226707
>>62221525
I don't know the fucking difference between a W2 and a 1099.

item.title: Capitalist Confessions thread — 8 replies — 8.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Capitalist Confessions thread

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-13 18:22 UTC

OP

> be me
> open liquidation bin store after watching 3 entrepreneur tiktoks
>Rent space in strip mall by airport
> tell everybody we are “bringing deals back to the community”
> buy truckload of amazon returns and temu overstock
> customers start actually finding deals
> problematic development
> lady walks up on $4 day holding sealed 30-pack of pillowcases
> cashier looks at me
> i look at cashier
> do quick mental math
> she is about to leave with 30 pillowcases for $4 total
> absolutely not
> rip package open in front of her like a starving coyote
> ”umm actually these are individual items”
> customer looks like she just witnessed a crime
> put up sign next morning
> ”MULTIPACKS PRICED INDIVIDUALLY”
> laminate it
> feel like a genius businessman
> start noticing customers happy when they find electronics
> cannot allow this
> put everything remotely valuable behind glass cases
> including dusty returned air fryer missing tray
> and bluetooth speaker that smells like cigarettes
> price all of it higher than amazon
> customer literally pulls up cheaper amazon listing on phone
> tell him “yeah but ours is local”
> he leaves immediately
> tell people we are “competing with dollar stores”
> forget that dollar stores are cheap
> store slowly transforms from treasure hunt to depressing pawn shop energy
> customers stop digging through bins with excitement
> now they just sigh heavily and leave
> business slowing down mysteriously
> buy giant neon sign that says TREASURE HUNT
> surely this will fix everything

Early replies (first 3)

62225841
I dont give a fuck, wagie.

62225853
>>62225841
I own my store, dumbass. Sounds like you're projecting onto me.

62225878
>>62225853
You are the biggest retail wagie of all, you work and all of your capital is at risk.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62225986
>>62225827
Ghost? Have you finally come clean?

62226237
>>62225827
Just let them have whatever for cheap. How much does it cost you per pallet? How much do you clear selling it cheap?

It all SHOULD be too cheap to say no. That's the point.

62226438
>>62225827
All of this sounds plausible. Op is retarded per usual. Some people need to learn the hard way.

You have to actually put deals in there. It's like a slot machine, you tucking moron.

62226593
>>62225827
IM SHOOTIN' PEARLS HERE

62227141
>you can buy seeds with EBT
>me and pookie have been using an abandoned lot in the hood to grow produce
>mfw Pell Grant is paying for me to learn agricultural science at community college
>harvest time comes around
>we bring it all back to the hood
>make 10x what we got with our SNAP cards
>buy raw coco with the money and cook it
>slang that shit on the first of the month for more ROI
I want to thank all the capitalists and tax payers out there. Without y'all niggas I wouldn't be living the ghetto capitalist dream I'm living.

item.title: Australia is fucked — 23 replies — 5.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Australia is fucked

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-05-13 14:54 UTC

OP

>CGT discount - DENIED
>Negative Gearing - DENIED
Where were you when the Australian dream died?

Early replies (first 3)

62224090
have the liberals said they will reverse it? do we just hold and wait?

62224122
>>62223850
Doesn’t this come into effect in 2027? Should I sell all my crypto before 2027?

62224148
>>62223850
its tiring living here
the new rules favour low return long duration assets. high growth assets like btc and leveraged etfs get smoked compared old rules. run a few tests... under 12 months nothing changes.
i wonder if it actually encourages speculation. might as well punt on options or asx bets miners or U.S letfs... you need BIG wins now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224204
>>62223850
Negative gearing has always been retarded.

62224281
>>62223850
Explain whats changed so i can care or not ?
t aussie

62224288
>>62223850
>the Australian dream
KEK
Australia was a prison land where we sent the scum to so they can starve or kill each other.
Only after the jews mettled did they get rights.

62224331
>>62224281
No more long term CGT discount. Minimum taxed is now always 30% even if you used to be in lower bracket

62224341
>>62224288
>where we sent
I really hope this isn't a bong posting, because lol if you think bongs are in any position to be talking like this in current year.

62224346
>>62224288
Shalom!

62224365
Relax, the changes only apply to new purchases, as long as you already have your properties you'll be paying less tax than future property owners.

62224398
>>62224346
>doesn't even reach half a billion
LOL

62224409
CGT changes for all asset types is retarded and makes it so property is still the best investment
My cope is that negative gearing changes will result in cheaper properties but higher yields as rental supply drops

62224440
>>62224365
dont care about properties, i want to know if shares are grandfathered in

62224467
>>62223850
SOMEONE EXPLAIN THIS TO ME IN ENGLISH
JUST HOW COOKED ARE WE CUNTS???

62224496
>>62224331
Right thanks m8.

62224499
>>62224440
This, except crypto and boomer rocks.

62224536
>>62224467
Return to pre-1999 CGT system.

62224566
>>62224536
CGT is theft, Fuck you albo.

62224682
>>62223850
I voted for this.

62224702
>>62224536
but... w why they do this do this to us

62224712
I'm of two minds on one hand I have about 600k AUD of cgt obligations which if I were to use the discount while I can would have to lock in maybe 100k of tax this coming year (yes I'll be at 45% bracket because I'm not a brokie)

On the other hand I'm priced out of the property market (not really but I don't want to pay 900k for a shitty Sydney apartment) that any fucking over of property investors is actually a good thing (for me and people my age)

62224719
>>62224712
Doesnt making $600k in profit automatically put you in the highest tax bracket owed regardless of how much you earned from waging?

62226023
Honestly, everyone should be sticking their money into classic cars, right this instant.

Buy a final edition V8 Landcruiser 70 series, a MK V Supra or a Y62 Patrol Warrior and just sit on it.

Watch this space, its exempt from CGT and may sky rocket in value, especially as we push forward into cucked out 4cyl and 3cyl hybrid cuckboxes.

My cars will now become my investments, fuck the Government.

item.title: Nintendo Criminally Undervalued — 89 replies — 4.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo Criminally Undervalued

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-05-13 00:24 UTC

OP

Honestly believe Nintendo is grossly undervalued. Considering a buy at current levels. Thing is, I’ve never bought an ADR before. What are the advantages/disadvantages of buying an ADR

Early replies (first 3)

62212154
>>62212149
Not buying that bing bing wahoo slop. Sorry.

62212158
>>62212149
I tried this when it dropped to 16. Stopped out at 12. I would let it bleed for a while might go lower.


About ADRS… beats buying on the tokyo exchange. Its 100 shares minimum on tokyo. Adr you can buy any amount. Only disadvantage is lower liquidity. If you want the actual shares use ibkr for tokyo, otherwise NTDOY is fine.

62212161
>>62212149
Nintendo was trading around 3 dollars during the wii u saga. It can easily drop another 50% from here.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62213657
>>62213569
I'd buy at the 5 range

62213925
Crashing 12%
LMFAO LOL

62213949
>below LINK
Ohhhhhh Nintendieeeeeeeeeees

62213977
>>62213235
>You are aware that shares can go to zero right?
>"I don't mind it going to zero"
Do you know how to read? More upside doesn't mean stock will bounce up and down. It means the reward ratio of it being 0 is close to impossible due to it being backed by Tencent, having enough equity to pay off the debt, having IP's 4 times worth more than the debt.

>>62213213
>Gamers are so fucking stupid it's unreal. Ubisoft is a dying company, 9/10 of their projects don't make enough money
Wow what thoughtful response. Except it's not 9/10. It's been the last 4 games due to them being complacent with selling 7/10 games which made them a lot of money for a time. The entire company has restructured, they've gotten old game devs, quality controller much stricter. You can easily see it in AC BF Resynced and majority of the game was still made under the old team. Also, Tencent isn't going to let their 800m (and additional 1 billion on IP) dollar investment just go into flames. Ubisoft made a deal with the board (including Tencent) to not be takeover by Tencent for 8 years in 2022 to allow for a turnaround. This deadline is in 4 years. Tencent will want to fully take over the company by 2030 and have every incentive to not allow the debters to take their investment and IP.

62213982
>>62213925
Nintendos Market cap is below Activision when it got bought out for 69Billion. I doubt Nintendo isn't worth 55B.

62214016
Gaming is dead. All new games are something redone and done again, remakes and remasters, nostalgia bait and whatever. A lot of old IP are being killed by woke shit. A lot of people are just buying emulation consoles and playing old games. The last console generation with a huge amount of good games was ps2 (just for example: gta3, gta vc, gta sa). After that, the games that people are playing like crazy are the ones that people are addicted for decades like cs and dota.

62214017
>>62214016
Games that have online multiplayer with micro transactions, that's why on ps2 we had 3 gta, but gta v had 3 playstations.

62214026
>>62212149
Not buying your bing bing wahoo. Simple as

62214263
>>62212192
>ubislop
Im sorry to hear that

62214411
>>62212203
How do you think the stat of a gorillion % of stockpickers underperform arose.

62215019
>>62212149
bing bing wahoo slop top

62215031
https://www.zerohedge.com/markets/memory-crunch-sends-nintendo-shares-deeper-bear-market

I told you so and don't run away from your public shaming OP.

62215101
>>62214263
Pretty sure Guillemot has ties to the French government. It'll be ok.

62215398
>>62215031
You don’t base your investment decisions off zerohedge slop, do you?

62215752
>>62215398
I used to, and yea they lost me a lot of money the damn Bulgarian Russian fucks.

62215887
>>62212149
>undervalued
How, why? Explain this without analysing price but the actual valuation of the company.

62215912
>>62213519
Lmao I can feel the desperation thought your post.
Next time buy s&p500 fag.

62215923
>>62215912
>Through your post
Damn spell checker

62215940
>>62215887
Nintendo is the cultural pillar of japanese economy.

62215953
I don't fully get it.
Nintendo makes most of its money from software sales right? That side is hardly affected by hw issues.
They might lose profits on console sales and the worst thing being diminished install base growth which affects the game sales.
Have they really been that overvalued?
They are not going anywhere and their games will continue selling millions.

62216426
>>62213213
It's why we see sony getting into cars. When IMO they need to get into appliances since the margins are better.

62217444
>>62213320
Yes, but so far Nintendo is going full hollywood and theme parks, which isn't ideal. They should go harder on other shit for the stock to go higher, Switch 2 sales are already in a decline.
>>62213977
>Except it's not 9/10. It's been the last 4 games due to them being complacent with selling 7/10 games which made them a lot of money for a time
>Last 4 games
Look at the list here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Ubisoft_games:_2020%E2%80%93present
I want you to look at the list here and dare if any of these are successful besides maybe AC Shadows, which isn't a smash hit as they would've wanted to get out of Tencent. If your only successful product line is from the AC series and nothing else, then you're looking at a company that's in the brink of ruin. Tencent will wait for Ubisoft to keep shooting itself in the foot until they get every worthwhile IP of theirs for cheap. I'm not saying this for you by the way, I'm saying this for everyone else not to be retarded and buy Ubisoft stocks.
>>62216426
Appliances are hard when they have stiff competition from Korea and China. Sony owns Music labels, animation studios and various many things but they still need to downsize to become more profitable. Their phones are non-existent, they're making joint operations with TCL on TVs now. If anything they need to look at Panasonic and just downsize and focus on extremely profitable industries and not bother with anything else.

62217766
>>62212192
>unironically Ubisoft
zamn

62219639
>>62217444
Kino reply. Got any more info on Sony?

62219641
Nintendo neglects their core business.
It's fine to really want to make money on licensing. But it's not fine to let the game's unit languish. They deserve to be beaten down.

62219725
Zoomers and Alphas aren`t buy trash like Milenials manchild.

Their games run better on android emulator than their own videogame.

62219799
>>62212612
Nintendo only owns a third of The Pokemon Company.

62221370
>>62219725
Good morning saar

62222931
>>62212192
the first half of your post is gold info
the 2nd half is poo poo

62223716
>>62217444
>I want you to look at the list here and dare if any of these are successful besides maybe AC Shadows

>The Division 2
10m Copies sold
>Crew 2
1.4M sold, above dev cost
>Assassin's Creed Valhalla
Best selling AC by far to date generating 1B in revenue (More than the current value of the company lol)
>Rainbow Six
Surpassed 100M registered players. Generatd 3.8B revenue since release.
30M active montly players.
>Far Cry 6
2m units sold on only steam. Game was profitable.
>Just Dance 2022S
Sold 4M globally
>Assassin's Creed Mirage
Total sales 5m with 5M on Ubisoft+
>Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora
Top selling Xbox games of 2024
>Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown
low cost metroidvania, sold like 1.3m
>Anno 117: Pax Romana
Was the fastest selling Anno series games.

I mean these are HUGE amounts of games that did well in only... 6 years?
>If your only successful product line is from the AC series and nothing else, then you're looking at a company that's in the brink of ruin.

That's flawed logic. The company needs to be continuing to go negative cashflow for it to be in the brink of ruin. Ubisoft had a few bad titles.

>Tencent will wait for Ubisoft to keep shooting itself in the foot
They already paid them half of the debt for 26% for 3 IP's with no control with buyback period. Ubisoft will do fine with the recontruction they're doing, calling old devs and new QC standards. It already shows in Black Flag. They have 45 Studios around the world with 2 billion in revenue. They can easily fire people and sell assets to pay off the debt.


>I'm not saying this for you by the way, I'm saying this for everyone else not to be retarded and buy Ubisoft stocks.
We'll see who'll have the laugh by July 9th. It's the most shorted stock in France atm, there will be a short squeeze to back to book value at least once we see the reviews, total sales and stuff. Screen Cap this.

item.title: goylife — 8 replies — 4.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
goylife

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-13 17:18 UTC

OP

>check my gmail
>ad ad ad ad
>check my PHYSICAL MAIL
>ad ad ad
>ride the metro and look up in the train cart
>ad ad ad ad
>open phone to literally any app or website
>ad ad ad ad

im just an ad viewing machine im not even human

Early replies (first 3)

62224887
>>62224886
>watch ad
>buy this!
>i have no money

62224891
>open website
>advert autoplays
>ACCEPT OUR COOKIES SO WE CAN SELL DATA TO COMPANIES
>newsletter
>ai chatbot messanger in the corner
>giant scrolling ads when you scroll down

62224896
>>62224891
brave?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224923
NEET lifetime earnings: $50.27
NEET lifetime hours watching ads: 18,561

62225074
>>62224886
Ok true but not even 1 ad in 1000 ads nudge me to buy something so these companies pay for nothing while i dont need to pay for the website.

62225093
The entire economy is built on ads and subscription plans to avoid ads, but even those get ads added to them eventually, but you can always upgrade to the new premium + package for an ad-free experience.

62225624
>>62224891

62226783
>>62224891

item.title: >There are people >Like REAL people >These real people spend 8 hours a day 40 days a week having to answer to entitled c… — 5 replies — 3.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
>There are people >Like REAL people >These real people spend 8 hours a day 40 days a week having to answer to entitled c…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-13 17:42 UTC

OP

>There are people
>Like REAL people
>These real people spend 8 hours a day 40 days a week having to answer to entitled customers directly directly to their face
>They have to smile. Like it's not a choice
>They have to do the most mundane and repetitive tasks and same phrases nonstop like some sort of low quality automation
>They don't own anything in these jobs
>They are more disposable than the items they their place sells
>They can't talk back to the boss or risk getting fired on the spot
>They have to wear demeaning uniforms that rob them of their individuality
>Their boss is always right, the customer is always right. They are always wrong. 2+2 LITERALLY equals 5 for them
>When they get home, their too drained to do anything but eat quick, unhealthy foods and doomscroll for hours until they go to sleep and restart the cycle
>They don't escape this
>No, rather things only get worse with children, financial repression, general entropy and so on
>They don't even dare to dream of the future. They just take their Kafkaesque nightmare "one day at a time" as a cope because they know they can't actually afford the rent in that's due in 13 days. Something they will probably answer with by going more into debt
There has to be something more to the story....r-right?

Early replies (first 3)

62225238
>>62225217
I dont feel bad at all because they seethe and defend the system, cope to no end. Eat shit normies

62225254
>>62225217
There are many ways to become one of them. By questioning the system you made the first step.
>>62225238
Resentment is not the way to profit. Embrace and nourish them and they will do everything for you.

62225646
>>62225217
gotta pay the bills somehow

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62226672
>>62225217
Just love yourself for no reason. Go to the gym. Run 8 mph. Go to church two times a month. Take a day off and go to the museum, celebrate the ancient cities. Reject modernity. Stay in the game.

62226927
>>62225238
They've got to get MAD
but they don't
they just take it and move on
like the cattle they are
I stopped being sympathetic to normies in 2020
jews are kind of based actually
sick fucks and all, but normies are so much worse
